purpose: One real round of verifier-as-reward: strong API LLMs PROPOSE Lean 4 proofs; a REAL Lean kernel on RunPod VERIFIES them. Tests the verifier_synthesis_over_proof_kernel premise end-to-end on non-trivial theorems.

compute.note: proposers were API models (Claude-Sonnet via LLMHub, DeepSeek via OpenRouter); verifier was Lean 4.31.0 on the pod; results via the pod HTTPS proxy; pod deleted after.

results.name: reverse_reverse
results.error:
openrouter_deepseek_deepseek-chat__reverse_reverse.lean:5:10: warning: Possibly looping simp theorem: `List.reverse.eq_1`

Note: Possibly caused by: `List.reverseAux_eq` and `List.reverse`

Hint: You can disable a simp theorem from the default simp set by passing `- theoremName` to `simp`.
openrouter_deepseek_deepseek-chat__reverse_reverse.lean:5:4: error: Tactic `simp` failed with a nested error:

case cons
a : Type

x : a

ih : (xs ++ ys).length = xs.length + ys.length
⊢ xs.length + ys.length + 1 = xs.length + 1 + ys.length

claims.shows: A real Lean 4.31.0 kernel verified 8/10 LLM-proposed proofs, including non-trivial induction proofs (reverse_reverse, length_append, map_map). The verifier-as-reward loop works end-to-end on real compute: propose (API LLM) -> verify (real kernel) -> keep accepted. The 2 rejections (a looping simp, an unsolved goal) were correctly caught.
claims.doesNotShow: This does NOT close the bet as a NOVELTY result. The 5 theorems are KNOWN library lemmas; the accepted proofs are RECALL of standard proofs, not novel discoveries. The cross-proposer Jaccard for map_map is 0.948 (both models emit the same standard proof). The repo novelty probe measures only surface overlap vs a LOCAL corpus and cannot detect training-data memorization. Closing the bet needs a HELD-OUT theorem provably outside the proposers' memorized corpus, with novelty verified semantically and reproduced across seeds under the no-overclaim gate.
